subject: Statistics
title: Why Bayes Was Ignored for 200 Years
description: One-minute skill: Why Bayes Was Ignored for 200 Years.

hook.text: Bayes' theorem was published in 1763. It didn't become mainstream statistics until the 1990s. Why the wait?

buildup.text: The math was intractable. Bayesian calculations require integrals that can't be solved by hand for real problems.

discovery.text: Computers and MCMC — Markov Chain Monte Carlo — changed everything. Suddenly, Bayesian models were computable.

twist.text: The frequentist school dominated because its methods were computationally cheap. Convenience won for two centuries.

climax.text: Now that computation is abundant, Bayesian methods are spreading across every field of science.

punchline.text: Bayes waited 200 years for computers. Now it's everywhere.

quiz.question: What technological advance enabled the Bayesian revolution?
options: Computers and MCMC algorithms that could solve previously intractable Bayesian calculations
